016package org.consensusj.bitcoin.jsonrpc.internal;
017
018import org.bitcoinj.core.Context;
019import org.consensusj.bitcoin.jsonrpc.BitcoinClient;
020import org.slf4j.Logger;
021import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
022
023import java.util.concurrent.ThreadFactory;
024
025
026/**
027 * A {@link ThreadFactory} for the {@link BitcoinClient}. It is based upon <b>bitcoinj</b>'s
028 * {@link org.bitcoinj.utils.ContextPropagatingThreadFactory} but differs in a few ways:
029 * <ul>
030 *     <li>It takes a {@link Context} in the constructor rather than propagating the Context from the caller. We want
031 *     all threads in the pool to have the same context as the associated {@code BitcoinClient} and (more importantly)
032 *      the connected server.</li>
033 *     <li>Does not use <b>bitcoinj</b>'s {@link org.bitcoinj.utils.Threading#uncaughtExceptionHandler} mechanism.
034 *     (For now, it just logs the uncaught exceptions.)</li>
035 *     <li>Has no direct dependencies on Guava, though it does depend on <b>bitcoinj</b>.</li>
036 *
037 * </ul>
038 */
039public class BitcoinClientThreadFactory implements ThreadFactory {
040    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(BitcoinClientThreadFactory.class);
041    private final Context context;
042    private final String name;
043    private final int priority;
044
045    public BitcoinClientThreadFactory(Context context, String name, int priority) {
046        this.context = context;
047        this.name = name;
048        this.priority = priority;
049    }
050
051    public BitcoinClientThreadFactory(Context context, String name) {
052        this(context, name, Thread.NORM_PRIORITY);
053    }
054
055    @Override
056    public Thread newThread(final Runnable r) {
057        Thread thread = new Thread(() -> {
058            try {
059                Context.propagate(context);
060                r.run();
061            } catch (Throwable e) {
062                log.error("Exception in thread", e);
063                throw e;
064            }
065        }, name);
066        thread.setPriority(priority);
067        thread.setDaemon(true);
068        thread.setUncaughtExceptionHandler(BitcoinClientThreadFactory::uncaughtException);
069        return thread;
070    }
071
072    private static void uncaughtException(Thread t, Throwable e) {
073        log.error("Uncaught exception in thread: ", e);
074    }
075}
